items.title: Per-Harness Config Overrides
items.description: Let users define harness-specific overrides on top of the Claude Code base config — e.g., use a different MCP server list for Cursor, or disable certain rules for Aider. Right now, sync is all-or-nothing, forcing users to either accept imperfect parity or abandon sync entirely. Overrides let power users get 95% automation with 5% manual control where it matters.

items.title: Sync Profiles (Frontend, Backend, Data Science)
items.description: Pre-built sync profiles that configure what gets synced and how based on project type. A data science project might sync Jupyter-aware MCP servers and skip frontend skills; a backend project syncs DB tools. Solves the pain of manually curating what to sync per project context — most users just want a sensible default that matches their stack.

items.title: Import FROM Other Harnesses
items.description: Ingest config from Cursor, Windsurf, or Aider and translate it INTO Claude Code format — the reverse of the current flow. This is the migration story: users stuck in another harness can adopt Claude Code as source of truth without losing their existing investments. Massive acquisition funnel for new Claude Code users coming from Cursor.

items.title: Team Config Sync via Git Repo
items.description: Push synced configs to a shared git repo so entire teams stay in sync automatically. When one team member updates a skill or MCP config in Claude Code, others pull it down on next sync. Solves the 'we all have different AI setups' problem that plagues engineering teams — no more 'works on my harness' debugging.

items.title: Auto-Detect Newly Installed Harnesses
items.description: Watch for new AI harness installations (Cursor, Windsurf, Aider, etc.) and automatically prompt to configure and sync them. Today users must manually run /sync-setup for each harness. Discovery removes the onboarding friction — the moment you install a new tool, HarnessSync offers to wire it up.

items.title: Capability Gap Report Card
items.description: After each sync, generate a report showing what you LOST in translation — skills that have no equivalent, MCP tools that aren't supported, rules that got dropped. Today users have no visibility into what didn't sync. The report card makes gaps explicit and actionable, building trust that sync is complete or honest about its limits.

items.title: Selective Sync (Pick What to Sync)
items.description: Let users choose exactly which components sync to which harnesses — e.g., sync skills and MCP servers to Gemini but NOT rules; sync everything to Codex. Currently sync is binary per harness. Selective sync gives control to users who have harness-specific workflows they want to preserve.

items.title: Config Health Score Dashboard
items.description: Score the quality and completeness of each harness's synced config across dimensions: coverage (what % of Claude Code features translated), freshness (how stale vs source), consistency (drift from source). Gives users a single number to track 'how synced am I?' and surfaces the most impactful gaps to fix.

items.title: Auto-Generated Sync Changelog
items.description: After each sync, produce a human-readable changelog of exactly what changed: 'Added skill X to Codex, updated MCP server Y in Gemini, removed rule Z from Cursor.' Today syncs happen silently. A changelog builds confidence that sync did the right thing and creates an audit trail for teams.

items.title: Skill Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Show a matrix of every skill vs every harness, indicating which skills are fully supported, partially supported, or incompatible. Users currently discover incompatibilities at runtime when a skill fails in a non-Claude harness. A pre-flight matrix lets them make informed decisions about which harness to use for skill-heavy workflows.

items.title: MCP Server Reachability Checker
items.description: Before syncing MCP server configs, verify each server is reachable and responding. Syncing broken MCP configs to 5 harnesses multiplies a single misconfiguration into 5 broken tools. Pre-flight health checks catch this before it spreads and give users actionable 'server X is down' errors instead of mysterious tool failures.

items.title: Community Adapter Generator
items.description: An interactive wizard that generates the skeleton code for a new harness adapter — asking the user to describe the target harness's config format and file locations, then producing a working adapter stub. Unlocks community-contributed adapters for harnesses like Continue.dev, Plandex, or Mentat without requiring core team involvement.

items.title: Harness Version Compatibility Warnings
items.description: Track which versions of Codex, Gemini CLI, OpenCode etc. support which features, and warn users when they're on an old version that can't handle a synced config. Today, syncing fails silently or with cryptic errors when the target harness is outdated. Version-aware sync gives users a 'please upgrade Gemini CLI to 1.4+' message instead.

items.title: Secure Env Var Sync Across Harnesses
items.description: Sync environment variable configurations across harnesses while integrating with secrets managers (1Password CLI, macOS Keychain, or .env files). Each harness has different env var config formats. Today users manually duplicate env var setup per harness — and often forget to update all of them when rotating credentials.

items.title: Drift Alerts via Notification Center
items.description: Send macOS notifications when a harness config drifts from the Claude Code source — e.g., if someone manually edits a Gemini config file directly. Today drift is silent until the next /sync-status check. Proactive alerts keep users aware that their 'synced' state has been broken without requiring manual polling.

items.title: Portable Config Bundle Export/Import
items.description: Export your entire HarnessSync setup — source config, adapter settings, sync profiles — as a single portable .tar.gz bundle that can be imported on a new machine. Solves the 'set up a new laptop' problem that takes hours of manual harness configuration. One import command restores everything.

items.title: GitHub Actions / CI Sync Integration
items.description: A GitHub Action that runs HarnessSync in CI to validate that committed Claude Code config would sync cleanly to all configured harnesses. Catches sync regressions before they reach teammates. For teams treating their AI config as infrastructure-as-code, this closes the loop on config quality in PRs.

items.title: Task-Based Harness Recommendations
items.description: Based on the current task type (frontend, SQL query, infra, etc.), recommend which synced harness is best suited for that task given the config each has available. Developers waste time switching harnesses manually or defaulting to one harness for everything. Smart routing surfaces 'for this data pipeline task, Gemini has better MCP coverage.'

items.title: Visual Side-by-Side Sync Diff
items.description: A rich terminal UI showing a side-by-side diff of what a sync would change — source on the left, target on the right, with color-coded additions/removals. The current /sync-diff is text-based and hard to parse. A visual diff makes it fast to spot unintended changes before applying them.

items.title: Org-Wide Config Policy Enforcement
items.description: Define organization-level policies (e.g., 'all harnesses must have X MCP server', 'skill Y is forbidden') that HarnessSync enforces on every sync across all team members. Enterprise teams need to ensure AI tools comply with security policies. Without enforcement, individual developers quietly misconfigure their harnesses.

items.title: Smart Idle-Time Background Sync
items.description: Instead of syncing immediately on every change (which can be disruptive), detect developer idle time and batch-sync then. Reduces the 'sync interrupted my workflow' problem. Similar to how Time Machine waits for disk activity to settle — sync when the developer takes a break, not mid-keystroke.

items.title: Community Config Template Marketplace
items.description: A curated library of HarnessSync config templates contributed by the community — 'React dev setup', 'Python ML engineer', 'DevOps / infra' — that users can import as a starting point. New users face a blank slate when setting up Claude Code. Templates dramatically reduce time-to-value and surface best practices.

items.title: Point-in-Time Rollback
items.description: Keep a rolling history of sync snapshots (last 30) and let users roll back any harness to any previous state with a single command. Today, a bad sync that breaks Gemini config requires manual recovery. Snapshots make recovery a one-liner, reducing the risk of experimenting with config changes.

items.title: PR Comment Sync Preview
items.description: When opening a PR that changes Claude Code config files, automatically post a comment showing what the sync impact would be across all harnesses — which harnesses gain/lose capabilities. Gives reviewers and authors immediate visibility into the downstream impact of config changes before merge.

items.title: Auto-Generated Harness-Optimized Skill Variants
items.description: When a skill uses Claude Code-specific syntax that other harnesses don't support, automatically generate a harness-native variant that approximates the same behavior. Today unsupported skills just don't sync. Variants mean users get degraded-but-functional versions everywhere instead of nothing.

items.title: Cloud Config Backup (GitHub Gist / iCloud)
items.description: Automatically back up the full Claude Code config to a private GitHub Gist or iCloud Drive after each successful sync. Protects against accidental deletion and enables fast recovery on new machines without needing a full git repo. One command restore from cloud backup.

items.title: Custom Config Lint Rules
items.description: Let users (or orgs) define custom lint rules for their Claude Code config — e.g., 'skills must have a description', 'MCP servers must have a health check endpoint configured'. The existing /sync-lint checks for format issues; custom rules enforce team standards and best practices that vary by organization.

items.title: Multi-Workspace Sync Manager
items.description: Manage sync across multiple project directories from a single control plane — see all workspaces, their sync status, and trigger cross-workspace syncs. Power users with 10+ projects each have separate Claude Code configs that diverge. A multi-workspace view makes maintaining consistency across projects tractable.

items.title: Harness Update Feed (What's New)
items.description: Monitor harness release notes and notify users when a harness update unlocks better sync fidelity — e.g., 'Gemini CLI 1.5 now supports native MCP, re-sync to get full coverage.' Today users miss improvements because they don't track every harness's changelog. An update feed makes the value of upgrading visible.

items.title: Sync Webhooks for External Automation
items.description: Fire webhooks to external URLs after sync events — enabling integration with Slack bots, n8n workflows, or custom dashboards. Teams that use HarnessSync as part of a larger AI infrastructure automation pipeline need events they can react to. Webhooks make HarnessSync a composable piece of a larger system.

items.title: AI-Powered Config Quality Suggestions
items.description: Use Claude to analyze your current config and suggest improvements: missing MCP servers for your tech stack, skills that could be combined, rules that conflict. Goes beyond format validation to semantic config quality. Helps users get more out of their AI setup by surfacing blind spots they didn't know they had.

items.title: Cross-Harness Skill & Config Search
items.description: Search across all synced harness configs from a single command — find which harnesses have a given skill, rule, or MCP server. When debugging 'why does Gemini behave differently?', users currently grep through config files manually. A unified search surface makes cross-harness debugging instant.

items.title: Interactive First-Run Onboarding Wizard
items.description: A step-by-step TUI wizard that runs on first install: detects installed harnesses, explains what will be synced, shows a preview of changes, and asks for confirmation before applying. First-run is currently the highest-friction moment. A wizard converts skeptical users into confident ones by showing exactly what will happen before they commit.

items.title: Config Annotation System for Sync Intent
items.description: Allow users to annotate Claude Code config sections with sync intent markers — `# sync: codex-only`, `# sync: skip`, `# sync: adapt` — to control sync behavior inline. Today sync intent lives in separate config; inline annotations make it visible next to the config it affects and dramatically reduce context-switching.

items.title: Harness Response Quality Comparator
items.description: Run the same prompt against multiple synced harnesses and display responses side by side, so users can empirically compare quality given identical configs. Answers the question 'does my synced Gemini config actually behave like Claude Code?' with evidence instead of assumption. Especially useful after a complex sync to validate parity.

items.title: Sync Conflict Resolution for Team Configs
items.description: When multiple team members edit Claude Code configs independently (e.g., in different branches), HarnessSync detects conflicts and presents a merge UI before syncing. Teams using HarnessSync with shared git configs will inevitably hit this. Conflict resolution prevents one member's sync from silently overwriting another's changes.

items.title: Harness Feature Request Tracker
items.description: Track config features you wish a specific harness supported, and get notified when that harness releases support. When a user sees a capability gap (e.g., 'Codex doesn't support MCP servers'), let them subscribe to 'notify me when this changes.' Turns frustrating gaps into anticipated upgrade moments.

items.title: MCP Tool Stub Generation for Non-MCP Harnesses
items.description: When syncing to harnesses without MCP support, auto-generate shell script stubs that approximate MCP tool behavior (e.g., a bash script that wraps the same underlying API). Users who rely on MCP tools in Claude Code get at least degraded-but-functional equivalents elsewhere.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.

items.title: MCP Tool Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Show which MCP tools work in which harnesses and flag tools that will silently fail. Users waste hours debugging why an MCP capability works in Claude Code but not in Gemini.
